km.Penhow Castle, Newport, Monmouthshire, W: នែwប៉ូរត, ម៉ូនម៉ូuthស៊ីរe, វ៉ាឡិស

zh.lifeSummary: Lady Bartholomew de Saint Maur於1150年之前與Bartholomew Saint-Maur在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Bartholomew de Saint Maur于1150年之前与Bartholomew Saint-Maur在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Maur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ernesto, Ramon, Adela, Carlos, Eladio, Emilia, Estrella, Horacio, Jorge, Jose, Juan, Mario.  Italian Alessandra, Angelo, Cecilio, Cosimo, Gianfranco, Lorenzo, Mauro.
Catalan: from the personal name Mauri, Latin Maurus (see Mauro ).
Catalan and French (Occitan): probably from the personal name Malric (ancient Germanic Almaricus).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

nameMeanings.name: Bartholomew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of New Testament origin, the name of an apostle mentioned in all the synoptic gospels (Matthew, Mark, and Luke) and in the Acts of the Apostles. It is an Aramaic formation meaning ‘son of Talmai’, and has been assumed by many scholars to be a byname of the apostle Nathaniel . Talmai is a Hebrew name, said to mean ‘abounding in furrows’ (Numbers 13:22).

en.lifeSummary: Lady Bartholomew de Saint Maur married Bartholomew Saint-Maur before 1150, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Bartholomew de Saint Maur a épousé Bartholomew Saint-Maur avant 1150, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Bartholomew de Saint Maur hat vor 1150 in Europa Bartholomew Saint-Maur geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Bartholomew de Saint Maur lei sposato Bartholomew Saint-Maur prima del 1150, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Penhow Castle, Newport, Monmouthshire, W: ウェールズモンモウスシュアイアニュープオルト
ja.lifeSummary: Lady Bartholomew de Saint Maurは1150年以前にヨーロッパでBartholomew Saint-Maurと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Bartholomew de Saint Maur(은)는 1150년 이전 우럽에서 Bartholomew Saint-Maur(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Bartholomew de Saint Maur casou-se com Bartholomew Saint-Maur antes 1150, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Bartholomew de Saint Maur вышла замуж за человека по имени Bartholomew Saint-Maur перед 1150 г. в местности Европа. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Bartholomew de Saint Maur se casó con Bartholomew Saint-Maur antes de 1150, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
